opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My heart is racing out of my chest, my hands are shaking uncontrollably, and I feel like I'm having a heart attack. I know I have thyroid antibodies.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on after a stressful argument and I'm terrified it's my thyroid or my heart.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 30 minutes ago after a stressful event
symptom_narrative.character: racing heart, hand tremor, sense of impending doom, mild breathlessness
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: palpitations feel regular/fast; mild breathlessness; no radiating chest pain, no fainting, no drenching sweats, no fever

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an overactive-thyroid antibody condition (suppressed TSH, normal thyroid hormones, TSH-receptor antibodies)

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to be told her heart/thyroid are safe

objective_data_offered.value: my watch shows about 105 and it feels regular
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: wearable during acute anxiety; not a diagnostic ECG
